This street, lined with Italian restaurants, is the busiest in Little Italy.
It seems there isn't anything particularly unusual about this.
I've really gotten used to this street.
As usual, the usual
is running the shop. I wonder if it’s his personal business?
It feels cramped, but that’s often how newspaper stands are.
Italian, American, Chinese
Magazines from various countries are lined up in every available space.
Among all these magazines, some probably go out of print within a few months.
Hey there, buddy! You've been coming around a lot lately.
Yeah, just some personal business.
What, you still haven't got her to meet you?
It's not like that.
Hah.
Oh, buddy, you're surprisingly not popular, huh?
So what are you buying me today?
Buddy.
If you just came to disturb me, that’s not cool.
Buddy.
I don't want just a looky-loo, either.
Oh, hey, weren't you the one from before?
Thanks for your help back then.
Are you really not one of those media people?
No.
That’s good to hear.
Hah.
Ah.
Is something going on?
Well, actually, there’s this lady who claims to be a reporter from some Apple thing, and she’s being all pushy about wanting to meet Anna and wanting to know where she is.
A female reporter, you say?
Yeah, yeah. She’s got sharp eyes and is big in the chest, and man, she’s really unpleasant.
Could it be that woman named Eva Courtney?
Ah, yes, that’s it! Do you know her?
Not exactly a friend, but I’ve seen her around.
I wouldn't say anything bad, but you should stop associating with a woman like that. People might think you’re unpleasant, too.
Oh my, I’ve taken up more time than I should have. I need to hurry home. See you!
Oh right, speaking of which, there was another new incident.
New incident?
Yeah. They say they’re not sure if it relates to that case, but it seems like a
killer has come to New York.
A killer has come?
Whatever, they found a woman's corpse at the pier, and it was missing both hands.
Again, it was severed, huh?
That's right. The cuts were clean, right at the joints.
Sounds a bit suspicious, doesn’t it?
I see.
No matter what the public says, I believe the murders in New York are the work of the same guy as before.
It's a little abrupt to say that.
What I told you the other day is all there is to it.
If Anna's absence is related to Eva's actions...
Could it be about Meryl's daughter?
I'm worried about how long Anna has been gone. Where did she go and what for?
If Anna doesn't come back, my business is toast.
Crime in New York isn't exactly rare, but a female corpse without wrists?
I still feel like it’s connected to the series of incidents that followed in San Francisco, Los Angeles, and Detroit.
Still, where does this perpetrator plan to strike next?
Considering the past, he’ll probably move to another city.
I just hope the criminal gets caught soon.
A murder just happened in New York, too. Why do these incidents never stop?
Why do people have to kill each other?
Eva was visiting Anna?
What on earth for?
Wherever Eva goes, incidents follow.
Does that mean she might be onto Meryl's daughter?!
I really can’t afford for Eva to mess up my request.
Maybe it’s better to wait a bit before acting?
I checked the mailbox before ringing the bell, but as usual, it was stuffed with mail.
Do I need to come back again?
With a sigh, I muttered to myself and returned to the street.
Anna still hasn't come back. When will we be able to meet?
※※※ It's a bug. ※※※
Ｖ＝
